updates.title: Claude just added Fable 5 back to your subscription, but there's a catch
updates.summary: Following the US government lifting export controls on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models, Anthropic temporarily re-added Fable 5 access to Pro, Max, Team, and select Enterprise subscription plans — covering up to 50% of weekly usage limits. The inclusion window closes July 7, 2026, after which Fable 5 usage shifts to API-rate usage credits. A Claude Code lead engineer clarified that this is a capacity management measure, not a permanent removal; Anthropic intends to restore Fable 5 as a standard subscription benefit 'as soon as capacity allows.'
updates.accuracy_notes: Core facts corroborated by multiple outlets (Digital Trends, BleepingComputer, PCWorld, Hacker News thread). The July 7 cutoff date and the 'not permanent' clarification from a Claude engineer are consistent across sources. No red flags; story is grounded in Anthropic's public communications.

updates.title: Claude Developer Platform: Rate limits raised, tiers consolidated, cache diagnostics beta, and billing fix for refusals
updates.summary: Anthropic published a cluster of Claude Platform API updates around July 4-5. Rate limits were raised across all plans (Chat, Cowork, Claude Code, API), and the usage tier structure was simplified from multiple tiers down to three: Start, Build, and Scale — with Claude Sonnet and Haiku limits now matching Opus at every tier. Cache diagnostics launched in public beta, letting developers pass a diagnostics.previous_message_id field to receive a cache_miss_reason explaining where the prompt cache prefix diverged. Additionally, API requests that return stop_reason: 'refusal' without generating any output are no longer billed.
updates.accuracy_notes: All updates sourced from Anthropic's official release notes page and corroborated by Releasebot (releasebot.io/updates/anthropic/claude-developer-platform). No contradictions found across sources. The refusal-billing fix and cache diagnostics are specifically mentioned in multiple independent summaries of the July 4-5 release notes.

updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.201 & v2.1.200: Manual permission mode default, background-agent stability, and Sonnet 5 session fix
updates.summary: Anthropic released Claude Code v2.1.201 and v2.1.200 on July 3. The most significant change is that the default permission mode has been switched to 'Manual' across the CLI, VS Code, JetBrains, and --help output — meaning Claude Code will no longer act autonomously on file/system operations without explicit user approval by default. AskUserQuestion dialogs no longer auto-continue; users must opt into idle timeouts via /config. v2.1.201 also fixes a bug where Claude Sonnet 5 sessions incorrectly injected harness reminders via the mid-conversation system role. Numerous reliability fixes landed for background agents, plugin loading from git worktrees, screen-reader accessibility, voice dictation, and terminal rendering under tmux.
updates.accuracy_notes: Directly sourced from the official Claude Code changelog at code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog and cross-verified against the GitHub release history at github.com/anthropics/claude-code/releases. Version numbers, dates, and feature descriptions match verbatim. High-confidence, no red flags.
